1→"""Tests for erk stack list command.""" 2→ 3→from click.testing import CliRunner 4→ 5→from erk.cli.cli import cli 6→from erk_shared.gateway.git.abc import WorktreeInfo 7→from erk_shared.gateway.graphite.types import BranchMetadata 8→from tests.fakes.gateway.git import FakeGit 9→from tests.fakes.gateway.graphite import FakeGraphite 10→from tests.test_utils.env_helpers import erk_inmem_env 11→from tests.test_utils.output_helpers import strip_ansi 12→ 13→ 14→def test_list_stack_shows_branches_with_worktrees() -> None: 15→ """Test that list shows stack branches that have worktrees.""" 16→ runner = CliRunner() 17→ with erk_inmem_env(runner) as env: 18→ repo_dir = env.erk_root / env.cwd.name 19→ 20→ # Create FakeGit with worktrees 21→ git = FakeGit( 22→ worktrees={ 23→ env.cwd: [ 24→ WorktreeInfo(path=env.cwd, branch="main", is_root=True), 25→ WorktreeInfo(path=repo_dir / "feat-1", branch="feat-1", is_root=False), 26→ WorktreeInfo(path=repo_dir / "feat-2", branch="feat-2", is_root=False), 27→ ], 28→ }, 29→ current_branches={env.cwd: "feat-2"}, 30→ git_common_dirs={env.cwd: env.git_dir},

1→"""CLI tests for erk workflow run list command. 2→ 3→This file focuses on CLI-specific concerns for the list runs command: 4→- Command execution and exit codes 5→- Output formatting and display (status indicators, Rich table) 6→- PR-centric view with direct PR extraction and plan→PR fallback 7→ 8→The integration layer (list_workflow_runs) is tested in: 9→- tests/unit/fakes/test_fake_github.py - Fake infrastructure tests 10→- tests/integration/test_real_github.py - Real implementation tests 11→ 12→This file trusts that unit layer and only tests CLI integration. 13→""" 14→ 15→from __future__ import annotations 16→ 17→from datetime import UTC, datetime 18→from pathlib import Path 19→ 20→from click.testing import CliRunner 21→ 22→from erk.cli.commands.run.list_cmd import list_runs 23→from erk.cli.constants import DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_NAME, PR_ADDRESS_WORKFLOW_NAME 24→from erk.core.context import ErkContext 25→from erk_shared.gateway.git.abc import WorktreeInfo 26→from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.types import IssueInfo 27→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import PullRequestInfo, WorkflowRun 28→from tests.fakes.gateway.git import FakeGit 29→from tests.fakes.gateway.github import FakeLocalGitHub 30→from tests.fakes.gateway.github_issues import FakeGitHubIssues

1→"""Unit tests for run command shared utilities. 2→ 3→Tests the extract_plan_number and extract_pr_number helper functions 4→for parsing display_title formats. 5→""" 6→ 7→from erk.cli.commands.run.shared import extract_plan_number, extract_pr_number 8→ 9→ 10→def test_extract_plan_number_new_format() -> None: 11→ """Test parsing new format: '123:abc456' → 123.""" 12→ assert extract_plan_number("123:abc456") == 123 13→ assert extract_plan_number("1:x") == 1 14→ assert extract_plan_number("999:distinct-id-here") == 999 15→ 16→ 17→def test_extract_plan_number_old_format_returns_none() -> None: 18→ """Test old format: 'Issue title [abc123]' → None.""" 19→ # Old format has title first, then distinct_id in brackets 20→ assert extract_plan_number("Add user authentication [abc123]") is None 21→ assert extract_plan_number("Fix bug in parser [xyz789]") is None 22→ 23→ 24→def test_extract_plan_number_with_colon_in_title_returns_none() -> None: 25→ """Test titles with colons but non-numeric prefix → None.""" 26→ # These look like they have colons but the prefix isn't a number 27→ assert extract_plan_number("Feature: Add caching [abc]") is None 28→ assert extract_plan_number("Bug fix: memory leak [xyz]") is None 29→ 30→
... [81 lines omitted] ...

1→"""Tests for PlanDetailScreen.execute_command.""" 2→ 3→from datetime import UTC, datetime 4→ 5→from erk.tui.data.types import PlanRowData 6→from erk.tui.screens.plan_detail_screen import PlanDetailScreen 7→from erk.tui.views.types import ViewMode 8→from tests.fakes.gateway.command_executor import FakeCommandExecutor 9→from tests.fakes.gateway.plan_data_provider import make_plan_row 10→ 11→ 12→class TestExecuteCommandBrowserCommands: 13→ """Tests for browser-related commands.""" 14→ 15→ def test_open_browser_opens_pr_url_when_available(self) -> None: 16→ """open_browser opens PR URL when PR is available.""" 17→ row = make_plan_row( 18→ 123, 19→ "Test", 20→ pr_url="https://github.com/test/repo/pull/456", 21→ plan_url="https://github.com/test/repo/issues/123", 22→ ) 23→ executor = FakeCommandExecutor() 24→ screen = PlanDetailScreen(row=row, executor=executor, view_mode=ViewMode.PLANS) 25→ screen.execute_command("open_browser") 26→ assert executor.opened_urls == ["https://github.com/test/repo/pull/456"] 27→ 28→ def test_open_browser_opens_issue_url_when_no_pr(self) -> None: 29→ """open_browser opens issue URL when no PR is available.""" 30→ row = make_plan_row(123, "Test", plan_url="https://github.com/test/repo/issues/123")
... [125 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant:
Perfect! Now let me create a comprehensive report with all the findings:
## Summary
I've successfully explored the test structure for CLI commands and TUI command availability in the erk codebase. Here's a comprehensive report:
---
## CLI Tests for Run Commands
### Test Files Location
**Primary test file:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/run/`
**Key test files:** - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/run/test_logs.py` - Tests for `logs_cmd.py` - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/run/test_list.py` - Tests for `list_cmd.py` - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/run/test_shared.py` - Tests for shared utility functions
### Test Structure Patterns for Run Commands
**1. Test Pattern (test_logs.py):** - Uses `Click.testing.CliRunner` for command execution - Uses dependency injection with fake gateways (`FakeGit`, `FakeLocalGitHub`) - Uses `create_test_context()` helper to construct test context - Uses `pytest` fixtures with `tmp_path` for filesystem isolation - Arrange-Act-Assert pattern
**Key pattern example:** ```python def test_logs_explicit_run_id(tmp_path: Path) -> None: # Arrange: Set up fakes repo_root = tmp_path / "repo" git_ops = FakeGit(...) github_ops = FakeLocalGitHub(run_logs={"12345": "logs"}) ctx = create_test_context(git=git_ops, github=github_ops, cwd=repo_root)
runner = CliRunner()
# Act: Execute command result = runner.invoke(logs_run, ["12345"], obj=ctx, catch_exceptions=False)
# Assert: Verify behavior assert result.exit_code == 0 assert "Step 1: Setup" in result.output ```
**2. Fixtures Used:** - `FakeGit` - Mocks git operations with `WorktreeInfo`, `current_branches`, `git_common_dirs` - `FakeLocalGitHub` - Mocks GitHub operations with `run_logs`, `workflow_runs`, `pr_plan_linkages` - `FakeGitHubIssues` - Mocks GitHub issues operations - `create_test_context()` - Helper to build ErkContext for testing
**3. Assertions:** - Exit codes (0 for success, 1 for errors, 2 for validation errors) - Output verification using `assert "text" in result.output` - Mutation tracking via fake's read-only properties
---
## TUI Command Availability Tests
### Test Files Location
**TUI command tests:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/tui/commands/`
**Key test files:** - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/tui/commands/test_registry.py` - Command registry and availability tests (~1036 lines) - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/tui/commands/test_execute_command.py` - Command execution tests - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/tui/app/test_command_palette.py` - Command palette integration tests
### Test Structure Patterns for TUI Commands
**1. Registry Tests (test_registry.py) - Core Pattern:** ```python def test_all_commands_have_unique_ids() -> None: """All commands should have unique IDs.""" commands = get_all_commands() ids = [cmd.id for cmd in commands] assert len(ids) == len(set(ids)), "Command IDs must be unique" ```
**Key functions tested:** - `get_all_commands()` - Returns complete list of all commands - `get_available_commands(ctx)` - Returns commands available in context - `get_display_name(cmd, ctx)` - Returns display name with context-specific values - `get_copy_text(cmd_id, ctx)` - Returns text to copy (or None if unavailable)
**2. Command Availability Tests:** The registry tests extensively validate availability conditions: ```python def test_open_pr_available_when_pr_url_exists() -> None: """open_pr should be available when PR URL exists.""" row = make_plan_row(123, "Test", pr_url="https://github.com/test/repo/pull/456") ctx = CommandContext(row=row, view_mode=ViewMode.PLANS) commands = get_available_commands(ctx) cmd_ids = [cmd.id for cmd in commands] assert "open_pr" in cmd_ids
def test_open_pr_not_available_when_no_pr() -> None: """open_pr should not be available when no PR URL.""" row = make_plan_row(123, "Test") # No PR URL ctx = CommandContext(row=row, view_mode=ViewMode.PLANS) commands = get_available_commands(ctx) cmd_ids = [cmd.id for cmd in commands] assert "open_pr" not in cmd_ids ```
**3. Fixtures Used:** - `make_plan_row()` - Factory function creating `PlanRowData` with optional fields - `CommandContext` - Context object with `row`, `view_mode`, `cmux_integration` flag - `ViewMode.PLANS` / `ViewMode.OBJECTIVES` / `ViewMode.LEARN` - View mode enum - `CommandCategory` - Category type (ACTION, COPY, etc.)
**4. Test Organization:** - Uses plain `def test_*()` functions (not test classes) - Organized by domain: availability tests, display name tests, shortcut safety, view mode filtering - ~1000+ lines of comprehensive test coverage
**5. Key Assertions:** ```python # Availability assertions assert "command_id" in cmd_ids assert "command_id" not in cmd_ids
# Display name assertions assert get_display_name(cmd, ctx) == "erk pr close 5831"
# Required field assertions assert cmd.id, f"Command missing id" assert cmd.name, f"Command {cmd.id} missing name" assert callable(cmd.is_available), f"Command {cmd.id} missing is_available"
# Shortcut uniqueness shortcuts = [cmd.shortcut for cmd in commands if cmd.shortcut is not None] assert len(shortcuts) == len(set(shortcuts)) ```
---
## General Test Patterns Across Both
### Common Testing Infrastructure
**Key helpers:** - `erk_inmem_env()` context manager - Sets up in-memory test environment - `env.build_context()` - Constructs ErkContext with defaults and overrides - `strip_ansi()` - Removes ANSI codes from output for assertions
**Fixture Parameters:** - `tmp_path: Path` - pytest fixture for temporary directories - `monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch` - For mocking/patching
### Exit Code Conventions - `0` = Success - `1` = Runtime error (git failure, data not found) - `2` = Validation error (invalid arguments)
### Organization Principles - Plain functions over test classes (unless testing class itself) - Single-file per command tests (unless 10+ tests justify splitting) - No raw `mock.patch` - use fake implementations instead - Constructor injection for all test state - Read-only properties for assertions on mutations
---
## Files for Cancel/Retry Commands
Based on the current branch (`plnd/add-run-cancel-retry-03-10-0757`), these commands are being added: - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/run/cancel_cmd.py` - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/run/retry_cmd.py`
You'll likely need to create corresponding test files following the same patterns: - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/run/test_cancel.py` - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/run/test_retry.py`
These should follow the exact pattern of `test_logs.py` and `test_list.py`.
